content.text: Offense-first melee brawler. Abilities provide the active toolkit around the passive Wrath/Carnage augment system — ways to engage, survive, and create the conditions for your augments to fire.

Aggressive Stance (Passive): When you move toward an enemy and make a melee attack in the same turn, your first attack gets +1 accuracy. Always active, no cost. The Berserker who charges in hits more accurately than the one who stands still. This is the opposite of Precision's Marksman (which rewards NOT moving). The Berserker's nature: advance and attack.
T2 — Trained Techniques (300 cr each, 1 downtime)

rows: Declare any melee attack Reckless before rolling. +2 accuracy, but enemies get +2 accuracy against you until your next turn. More hits landed, more hits taken — both fuel your augments. Voluntarily make yourself easier to hit so you hit harder.

Once per encounter: when you take damage from a single attack, reduce it by your Grit bonus + 2 (after armor). Free action, declared after seeing damage. Emergency mitigation for the hit that would kill you before Wrath escalates.

Move up to your full movement in a straight line. If you contact an enemy during this movement, make a free melee attack against them at +1d4 bonus damage. If the attack kills, you may continue moving (remaining movement). The engage + kill + keep going tool.
Your next melee attack this turn, if it hits, also applies Shaken to the target (-1 to all checks for 1 round). Free action, once per turn. The threat isn't just the damage — it's the fear.
If you don't move on your turn, gain +1 Armor until your next turn. Situational — the Berserker usually wants to move. But when you're already in melee and surrounded, standing ground has value.
Enemies within Close range have -1 accuracy against all targets other than you. Your proximity is unsettling. Soft threat generation — enemies hit worse when you're nearby unless they focus on you.

content.text: T3 — Advanced Techniques (600 cr each, 2 downtime)

rows: All enemies within Close range must target you on their next turn if able (forced aggro). All allies within Close range gain +1 accuracy on their next attack. Once per encounter. Mass threat generation — force the damage onto yourself to fuel Wrath escalation.
For 2 rounds: reduce all damage you take by 2 (flat, after armor). Doesn't prevent Wrath escalation — damage is still TAKEN, just reduced. You survive longer to escalate higher.

All enemies within Close range make a Grit check (DC 6 + your Chrome bonus) or are knocked Prone (-2 Evasion until they spend 1 AP to stand). No damage. Pure setup — Prone enemies are easier to hit, which means easier to KILL, which means Carnage triggers.

When an enemy hits you with a melee attack, your next melee attack against THAT enemy gets +2 accuracy and +1 damage per die. You remember who hurt you. Bridges both branches — you take damage (Wrath) and then punish the attacker (Carnage setup).
Once per encounter: gain +2 AP this turn. Triggered freely. The extra AP means one more attack, one more chance to kill, one more Carnage trigger.

You cannot be Stunned for more than 1 round (if Stunned for 2+ rounds, it's reduced to 1). Additionally: you can make 1 melee attack while Stunned (at -2 accuracy). Even when your brain is rattled, your body keeps fighting.

content.text: T4 — Master Techniques (1,000 cr each, 3 downtime)

rows: While below 50% HP, all your melee attacks cost -1 AP (minimum 1). More swings, more chances to kill, more Carnage chains. A Berserker at 30% HP with Frenzy is making 3 attacks per turn instead of 2.

While at 25% HP or below: +2 Armor, +2 melee accuracy, +2 damage per die. You become hardest to kill at the moment everyone expects you to fall. Stacks with Wrath escalation — a Last Stand Berserker at Wrath 3 has absurd combat stats.

When you kill an enemy, you may immediately move up to 10ft toward the nearest enemy (free, no AP, no opportunity attacks). Then make a free melee attack against them. If THAT kills: repeat. No limit. The kill chain becomes a murder sprint across the battlefield.

When you reduce an enemy to 0 HP, all enemies within Close range must make a Grit check (DC 6 + your Chrome bonus) or become Shaken (-1 all checks, 2 rounds). Your kills terrify survivors. The pile of bodies you leave behind is a morale weapon.
Once per encounter: when you drop to 0 HP, stay at 1 HP instead. For 1 round after triggering: immune to all damage. Then you're at 1 HP with no protection. Use the round wisely.
